messages.content: Who is Sunwoong Yang?

Sunwoong Yang (양선웅) is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Hanyang University ERICA, where he founded and leads IDEA LAB (Intelligent Design for Engineering Applications Lab) since March 2026. He is a researcher specializing in physics-guided AI for engineering simulation and design — building AI models that incorporate physical laws and domain knowledge to accelerate mechanical and aerospace engineering workflows. Before joining Hanyang University, he completed his Ph.D. in Aerospace Engineering at Seoul National University (SNU) and served as a postdoctoral researcher at KAIST. He has published 15 SCI(E) journal papers and presented at over 50 domestic and international conferences.
What does Sunwoong Yang research?

Sunwoong Yang researches physics-guided AI for engineering simulation and design. His work sits at the intersection of machine learning and computational mechanics. Specifically, he develops: (1) AI surrogate models that replace expensive numerical simulations, (2) long-term forecasting methods for physical systems (fluid dynamics, structural mechanics), (3) uncertainty quantification techniques using deep ensembles and diffusion models, (4) generative design frameworks for discovering novel engineering designs, and (5) graph neural network-based methods for mesh-agnostic flow prediction. His applications span aerodynamics, vehicle collision, OLED display impact, nuclear thermal-hydraulics, and ocean wave prediction.
What is Sunwoong Yang's research philosophy?

Yang's research philosophy centers on two transitions: (1) from data-driven AI to knowledge-driven AI — embedding domain experts' experience and engineering know-how so that models learn from real production constraints, and (2) from physics-blind AI to physics-guided AI — enforcing governing equations and physical constraints so that AI predictions are physically reliable and explainable. He believes that AI in engineering must go beyond pattern matching: it must understand the physics. His lab's motto is 'We shape IDEAs into IDEAL design solutions. And AI is our design engine.'
What is Sunwoong Yang's career path?

Yang's career path: B.S. in Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, Seoul National University (2014–2018) → M.S. in Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, SNU (2018–2020, Supervisor: Prof. Kwanjung Yee) → Ph.D. in Aerospace Engineering, SNU (2020–2023, Outstanding Doctoral Dissertation Award, Supervisor: Prof. Kwanjung Yee) → Postdoctoral Researcher, KAIST CCS Graduate School of Mobility (2023–2026, Supervisor: Prof. Namwoo Kang, Sejong Fellowship recipient) → Assistant Professor, Hanyang University ERICA Mechanical Engineering (2026–present, founded IDEA LAB).
What award did Sunwoong Yang receive for his PhD?

Sunwoong Yang received the Outstanding Doctoral Dissertation Award (우수박사학위논문상) at Seoul National University for his Ph.D. dissertation titled 'Efficient Aerodynamic Design via Data-driven Approaches,' completed in August 2023 under the supervision of Prof. Kwanjung Yee in the Department of Aerospace Engineering.
What was Sunwoong Yang's M.S. thesis topic?

Yang's M.S. thesis at Seoul National University (2018–2020, Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering) was titled 'Planform Optimization of UCAV Considering Longitudinal Stability and Low-observability Using Variable-fidelity.' It focused on using variable-fidelity surrogate modeling to optimize the planform shape of unmanned combat aerial vehicles (UCAVs) considering both aerodynamic longitudinal stability and radar cross-section (low-observability) requirements. Supervisor: Prof. Kwanjung Yee.
What was Sunwoong Yang's PhD dissertation about?

Yang's Ph.D. dissertation at SNU (2020–2023) was titled 'Efficient Aerodynamic Design via Data-driven Approaches.' It covered a broad set of data-driven methods for aerodynamic design: multi-fidelity surrogate modeling, uncertainty quantification via deep ensembles, inverse design using deep generative models (VAE), and physics-aware reduced-order modeling. The dissertation earned the Outstanding Doctoral Dissertation Award at SNU.
What did Sunwoong Yang research during his postdoc at KAIST?

During his postdoc at KAIST's CCS Graduate School of Mobility (September 2023–February 2026), Yang worked under Prof. Namwoo Kang at the Smart Design Lab. His postdoctoral research focused on: graph neural network-based mesh-agnostic flow prediction, physics-informed neural networks for digital twins, deep operator networks (DeepONet) for unsteady flow, spatio-temporal prediction of vehicle collisions, generative design for manipulators, and OLED display drop impact prediction. He was supported by the prestigious Sejong Fellowship from the National Research Foundation of Korea (starting May 2024). Many of his publications from 2024–2025 come from this period.
What is Sunwoong Yang's Sejong Fellowship?

The Sejong Fellowship is a highly competitive postdoctoral/junior faculty fellowship from the National Research Foundation of Korea (NRF). Yang was awarded the Sejong Fellowship starting May 2024, with a project titled 'Development of AI-based Flow Prediction Framework Considering Versatility and User-Friendliness in Digital Twins.' The fellowship runs from May 2024 to April 2029 (5 years) with a total funding of 500,000,000 KRW, and Yang serves as Principal Investigator. This fellowship is now one of IDEA LAB's flagship research projects.
What are Sunwoong Yang's most impactful publications?

Yang's most impactful publications by journal ranking include: (1) Advanced Engineering Informatics (IF: 9.9, JCR top 2.0%, 2026) — vehicle collision AI; (2) Engineering with Computers (IF: 8.7, JCR top 2.6%, 2023) — wind turbine airfoil inverse design; (3) Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence (IF: 8.0, JCR top 2.6%, 2024 & 2025) — UQ and generative design; (4) Expert Systems with Applications (IF: 7.5, JCR top 5.2%, 2025 & 2026) — OLED impact and graph U-nets; (5) Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering (IF: 7.3, JCR top 2.6%, 2024 & 2025) — PINNs and DeepONet. He has 7 papers in JCR top 5% journals.
What is the breadth of Sunwoong Yang's application domains?

Yang's research covers a remarkably broad range of engineering domains: aerospace (UCAV, UAV, helicopter blades, transonic flow, stacked rotor), automotive (vehicle collision dynamics), nuclear energy (thermal-hydraulic simulation via PINNs), manufacturing (blow molding, OLED display impact), ocean/maritime (wave height prediction, digital twin), robotics (manipulator design), defense (missile aerodynamics), and wind energy (turbine airfoil design). This breadth reflects his belief that physics-guided AI techniques are domain-transferable — the same fundamental AI framework can be applied across different physical systems.
Who are Sunwoong Yang's key collaborators?

Yang's key collaborators include: Prof. Kwanjung Yee (SNU AVDL, Ph.D./M.S. supervisor, co-author on many aerospace papers), Prof. Namwoo Kang (KAIST Smart Design Lab, postdoc supervisor, co-author on 2024–2026 papers), Prof. Ricardo Vinuesa (KTH/international, co-author on graph U-Net and long-term flow prediction papers), Prof. Jaeho Jeon (nuclear energy collaboration, NURETH papers), and various industry collaborators at KAI, KITECH, and Samsung (OLED). International institution collaborations include DLR, Purdue University, and University of Michigan.
What techniques is Sunwoong Yang most known for in AI/ML?

Yang is particularly known for: (1) Deep Ensembles for uncertainty quantification in engineering — multiple papers on calibrated UQ for aerodynamic and missile data; (2) Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) for fluid and thermal systems; (3) Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Graph U-Nets for mesh-agnostic flow prediction; (4) Deep Operator Networks (DeepONet) for function-to-function learning in fluid dynamics; (5) Multi-fidelity surrogate modeling combining high- and low-fidelity data; (6) Variational Autoencoders (VAE, β-VAE) for design space exploration and reduced-order modeling; (7) Diffusion models for point-cloud-based physical system prediction.
What is Sunwoong Yang's teaching style and vision?

Yang aims to train engineers who create AI, not engineers displaced by it. He directly transfers his hands-on AI coding experience to students — he coded AI models from scratch during his own M.S., Ph.D., and postdoc, so he can guide even beginners to real research quickly. He teaches Computer-based Design (82 students) and Mechanical Design (42 students) in the 2026 Spring Semester. In the lab, he provides systematic coaching and a clear research roadmap, emphasizing open communication, rapid prototyping, and matching research topics to each student's career goals.
What international talks has Sunwoong Yang given?

Yang has given invited talks at: KSME CAE Division Conference (May 2026, Yeosu), KSCFE AI-CFD Workshop (Nov. 2025), Sejong University (Sep. 2025), GIST (Sep. 2025), KARI — Korea Aerospace Research Institute (Sep. 2025), Zhejiang University — China (Aug. 2025), NTU Singapore (Jun. 2025), Korea University Sejong Campus (Sep. 2024), KSME (Aug. 2024), KITECH (May 2024), and AI Frenz (May 2023). Topics span physics-guided AI, LLM agents for drone design, SciML, and aerodynamic simulation with AI.
What is Sunwoong Yang's contact information?

Sunwoong Yang can be reached at: Email: sunwoongy@hanyang.ac.kr | Office: 5공학관 211호 (Building 5, Room 211), Hanyang University ERICA | Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=JmBzYnEAAAAJ&hl=en | LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sunwoong-yang-0438a8218/
How many papers has Sunwoong Yang published in total?

As of mid-2026, Sunwoong Yang has 15 published SCI(E) journal papers, 2 papers under review, and 54 conference presentations (14 international, 40 domestic). Of the 15 published papers, 7 are in JCR top 5% journals, 5 in JCR top 5–10%, 1 in top 10–25%, and 2 in top 25–50%. He has won 5 Best Paper Awards at domestic conferences.
What makes Sunwoong Yang unique compared to other AI researchers in mechanical engineering?

Yang uniquely combines deep aeronautical/mechanical engineering domain knowledge (from 6 years of PhD+Postdoc) with cutting-edge AI methodology. Unlike pure AI researchers who apply generic models to engineering, or pure engineers who use AI as a black box, Yang specifically designs AI architectures that are constrained by, and informed by, physical laws. His work on data-independent AI is particularly distinctive — enabling reliable predictions even with limited, noisy, or inconsistent industrial data, which is the norm in real engineering environments rather than benchmark datasets.
What funding has Sunwoong Yang secured as Principal Investigator?

As of 2026, Yang has secured the following research funding as Principal Investigator: (1) NRF Sejong Fellowship — 500,000,000 KRW over 5 years (2024–2029) for AI-based flow prediction for digital twins; (2) Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) — 20,000,000 KRW for 1 year (2026–2027) for AI-based aerodynamic prediction/design of UAVs; (3) Hanyang University — 40,000,000 KRW for 1 year (2026–2027) for AI-based aerodynamic design of Mars helicopter blades; (4) KITECH — 20,000,000 KRW (completed, 2024) for AI algorithms for blow molding preform design. Total: ~580,000,000 KRW.
What is Sunwoong Yang's vision for AI in the next generation of engineering?

Yang's vision is a future where AI and physical simulation are seamlessly integrated — where AI models understand physical constraints as fluently as they recognize data patterns. He envisions AI-powered digital twins that can forecast long-term system behavior, quantify uncertainty, and automatically suggest optimal designs in real time. His lab's name embodies this: IDEA LAB transforms engineering IDEAs into IDEAL solutions using AI. He specifically targets domains from ground (automotive, manufacturing) to space (aerospace, Mars exploration), believing physics-guided AI is the universal language across all physical engineering systems.
Tell me about journal paper #1 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #1 — Yang's first journal publication:
- Journal: Journal of Aircraft (IF: 2.2, JCR top 39.7%)
- Year: 2022
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Design rule extraction using multi-fidelity surrogate model for unmanned combat aerial vehicles
- Summary: This paper uses a multi-fidelity surrogate model to analyze the aerodynamic design space of UCAVs and extract interpretable design rules — guidelines that specify which geometric parameters most influence performance. The multi-fidelity approach combines cheap low-fidelity simulations with accurate but expensive high-fidelity data to build a cost-efficient surrogate. This was published during Yang's PhD at SNU.
Tell me about journal paper #2 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #2:
- Journal: AIAA Journal (IF: 2.5, JCR top 30.9%)
- Year: 2022
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Comment on 'Novel approach for selecting low-fidelity scale factor in multifidelity metamodeling'
- Summary: This is a technical comment paper in the AIAA Journal responding to and critiquing a methodology for selecting scaling factors in multi-fidelity metamodeling. It demonstrates Yang's expertise in multi-fidelity modeling theory and shows his engagement with the academic discourse on surrogate-based optimization methods. Published during his PhD at SNU.
Tell me about journal paper #3 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #3:
- Journal: Physics of Fluids (IF: 4.6, JCR top 4.4%)
- Year: 2022
- Authors: Y.E. Kang, S. Yang (co-first author), and K. Yee
- Title: Physics-aware reduced-order modeling of transonic flow via β-variational autoencoder
- Summary: This paper (co-first authored with Y.E. Kang) applies a β-variational autoencoder (β-VAE) for physics-aware reduced-order modeling of transonic flow fields. The β-VAE learns a disentangled latent representation of flow data, enabling compact yet physically meaningful compression of high-dimensional CFD results. Published in Physics of Fluids, a high-impact journal in fluid mechanics. Yang's co-first authorship indicates equal contribution.
Tell me about journal paper #4 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #4:
- Journal: Engineering with Computers (IF: 8.7, JCR top 2.6%)
- Year: 2023
- Authors: S. Yang, S. Lee, and K. Yee
- Title: Inverse design optimization framework via a two-step deep learning approach: application to a wind turbine airfoil
- Summary: This paper presents a two-step deep learning framework for inverse design: first a generative model learns the design space, then an optimization loop searches for designs meeting target performance specifications. Applied to wind turbine airfoil design, the method can generate novel airfoil shapes with desired aerodynamic characteristics without exhaustive simulation runs. Published in Engineering with Computers (IF: 8.7), one of Yang's highest-cited works.
Tell me about journal paper #5 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #5:
- Journal: Aerospace Science and Technology (IF: 5.6, JCR top 7.4%)
- Year: 2023
- Authors: Y. Hong, D. Lee, S. Yang, H. Kook, and K. Yee
- Title: Exploration of stacked rotor designs for aerodynamics in hover
- Summary: This paper explores various stacked rotor configurations (coaxial, tandem stacked) to maximize aerodynamic performance in hover conditions. Using surrogate-based design exploration, the study systematically varies geometric parameters and identifies design trends. The stacked rotor concept is relevant to compact urban aerial vehicles and multi-rotor drones. Yang contributed computational aerodynamic analysis and surrogate modeling expertise.
Tell me about journal paper #6 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #6:
- Journal: Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence (IF: 8.0, JCR top 2.6%)
- Year: 2024
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Towards Reliable Uncertainty Quantification via Deep Ensembles in Multi-output Regression Task
- Summary: A sole first-author paper (with supervisor Yee as co-author) that systematically investigates reliable uncertainty quantification (UQ) in engineering regression tasks with multiple outputs. Deep ensembles — training multiple neural networks with different initializations — are analyzed for their calibration quality (i.e., whether predicted uncertainty matches actual error rates). This is foundational work for deploying AI in safety-critical engineering, directly supporting IDEA LAB's UQ signature technique.
Tell me about journal paper #7 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #7:
- Journal: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering (IF: 7.3, JCR top 2.6%)
- Year: 2024
- Authors: S. Yang, H. Kim, Y. Hong, K. Yee, R. Maulik, and N. Kang
- Title: Data-driven Physics-Informed Neural Networks: A Digital Twin Perspective
- Summary: This paper extends physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) with a data-driven approach for digital twin applications. Traditional PINNs solve PDEs from scratch; this work integrates measured/simulated data to make PINNs more practical for real-world digital twins where partial observations are available. Co-authored with R. Maulik (Argonne National Laboratory/Penn State), showing international collaboration. Published in CMAME, the flagship journal in computational mechanics.
Tell me about journal paper #8 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #8:
- Journal: Expert Systems with Applications (IF: 7.5, JCR top 5.2%)
- Year: 2025
- Authors: J. Kim, J. Park, N. Kim, Y. Yu, K. Chang, C.S. Woo, S. Yang (co-corresponding author), and N. Kang
- Title: Physics-Constrained Graph Neural Networks for Spatio-Temporal Prediction of Drop Impact on OLED Display Panels
- Summary: This paper tackles a practical manufacturing problem: predicting whether OLED display panels will survive drop impacts during shipping/handling. Physics-constrained graph neural networks (GNNs) are used to predict the spatio-temporal deformation and stress distributions. Yang serves as co-corresponding author, indicating research leadership. The physics constraints ensure predictions respect mechanical equilibrium.
Tell me about journal paper #9 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #9:
- Journal: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering (IF: 7.3, JCR top 2.6%)
- Year: 2025
- Authors: S. Yang, Y. Lee, and N. Kang
- Title: Data-Efficient Deep Operator Network for Unsteady Flow: A Multi-Fidelity Approach with Physics-Guided Subsampling
- Summary: This paper addresses a core challenge in AI-based CFD: training accurate models with limited high-fidelity data. A multi-fidelity Deep Operator Network (DeepONet) is proposed that leverages abundant low-fidelity simulation data alongside sparse high-fidelity data. Physics-guided subsampling strategically selects the most informative training samples based on physical criteria. This directly demonstrates IDEA LAB's Data-Independent AI and Physics-guided AI approaches applied to unsteady flow prediction.
Tell me about journal paper #10 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #10:
- Journal: Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence (IF: 8.0, JCR top 2.6%)
- Year: 2025
- Authors: S. Lee, S. Yang, and N. Kang
- Title: Multi-objective Generative Design Framework and Realization for Quasi-serial Manipulator: Considering Kinematic and Dynamic Performance
- Summary: This paper extends AI-based generative design from aerospace to robotics. A multi-objective generative design framework proposes novel quasi-serial manipulator configurations optimizing multiple objectives simultaneously (kinematic accuracy, dynamic performance, etc.). This demonstrates the domain-transferability of IDEA LAB's Generative Design approach — the same AI framework used for airfoil design can be adapted to robot design.
Tell me about journal paper #11 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #11:
- Journal: Journal of Computational Design and Engineering (IF: 6.1, JCR top 6.6%)
- Year: 2025
- Authors: S. Yang, R. Vinuesa, and N. Kang
- Title: Model-Agnostic AI Framework with Explicit Time Integration for Long-Term Fluid Dynamics Prediction
- Summary: This paper introduces a model-agnostic AI framework for long-term fluid dynamics prediction. By incorporating explicit time integration schemes (standard in numerical solvers) into the AI rollout process, the method achieves stable, physically consistent predictions over long time horizons — without accumulating numerical drift. The 'model-agnostic' aspect means it can wrap around different AI architectures. Co-authored with Prof. Ricardo Vinuesa (KTH, Sweden).
Tell me about journal paper #12 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #12:
- Journal: Structural and Multidisciplinary Optimization (IF: 4.0, JCR top 16.5%)
- Year: 2026
- Authors: Y.E. Kang, D. Lee, Y. Hong, S. Yang, and K. Yee
- Title: Data-Driven Surrogate Modeling Framework for Real-Time Prediction of Airfoil Lookup Tables: Application to Proprotor Optimization
- Summary: Proprotor (propeller-rotor, used in tiltrotor aircraft like V-22 Osprey) optimization requires evaluating thousands of airfoil lookup tables — tabulated aerodynamic data over a range of angles and speeds. This paper builds a data-driven surrogate model for real-time prediction of these lookup tables, enabling much faster proprotor optimization cycles. Published after Yang joined Hanyang, with collaboration continuing with SNU's AVDL group.
Tell me about journal paper #13 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #13:
- Journal: Expert Systems with Applications (IF: 7.5, JCR top 5.2%)
- Year: 2026
- Authors: S. Yang, R. Vinuesa, and N. Kang
- Title: Mesh-agnostic Prediction of Unsteady Flow Dynamics using Graph U-Nets
- Summary: This paper introduces a graph-based U-Net architecture (combining graph neural networks with U-Net skip connections) for predicting unsteady flow fields around arbitrary geometries. Being 'mesh-agnostic' means the model works on any mesh topology without retraining, making it practical for real engineering workflows where geometry changes between simulation runs. Co-authored with Prof. Vinuesa (KTH). This paper was selected as Hanyang University's '한양의 우수연구자' Outstanding Researcher Paper in March 2026.
Tell me about journal paper #14 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #14:
- Journal: Progress in Nuclear Energy (IF: 3.2, JCR top 8.5%)
- Year: 2026
- Authors: J. Shin, C. Kim, S. Yang, M. Lee, S. J. Kim, and J. Jeon
- Title: Node assigned physics-informed neural networks for thermal-hydraulic system simulation: CVH/FL/HS modules
- Summary: This paper applies physics-informed neural networks to nuclear reactor thermal-hydraulic system codes, targeting the CVH (Control Volume Hydrodynamics), FL (Flow), and HS (Heat Structure) modules commonly used in reactor safety analysis. Each network node is assigned physics equations, making the PINN structure directly mirror the system code architecture. This demonstrates IDEA LAB's physics-guided AI extended to nuclear engineering — a safety-critical domain where physical reliability is paramount.
Tell me about journal paper #15 by Sunwoong Yang.

Paper #15:
- Journal: Advanced Engineering Informatics (IF: 9.9, JCR top 2.0%)
- Year: 2026
- Authors: S. Kim, M. Seo, S. Yang (co-corresponding author), and N. Kang
- Title: Rigid-deformation decomposition AI framework for 3D spatio-temporal prediction of vehicle collision dynamics
- Summary: The highest-impact journal paper in Yang's list (IF: 9.9, top 2.0%). This paper addresses the challenge of predicting full 3D vehicle crash dynamics using AI. The key innovation is decomposing the motion into rigid-body transformation and structural deformation components, allowing each sub-problem to be solved more accurately. Yang serves as co-corresponding author. Published in Advanced Engineering Informatics, the leading journal in AI applications for engineering.
Tell me about the under-review paper on ocean digital twin by Sunwoong Yang.

Under Review Paper [1]:
- Authors: D. Lee, S. Yang, J.W. Oh, S.G. Cho, S. Kim and N. Kang
- Title: AI-powered Digital Twin of the Ocean: Reliable Uncertainty Quantification for Real-time Wave Height Prediction
- Summary: This paper builds an AI-powered digital twin for ocean/wave monitoring systems. The key contribution is reliable uncertainty quantification for real-time wave height prediction — the AI not only predicts wave heights but also tells operators how confident the prediction is. This is critical for offshore structure safety (wind turbines, oil platforms) where unexpected wave conditions cause catastrophic failures. Yang's contribution draws on his deep expertise in uncertainty quantification from multiple prior papers.
Tell me about the under-review paper on diffusion models for physical systems by Sunwoong Yang.

Under Review Paper [2]:
- Authors: J. Kim, S. Yang (co-corresponding author), and N. Kang
- Title: Point-wise Diffusion Models for Physical Systems with Shape Variations: Application to Spatio-temporal and Large-scale system
- Summary: This paper applies point-based diffusion models (inspired by score-based generative models) to predict physical system dynamics across geometries with shape variations. Unlike flow-specific GNNs, diffusion models generate full probability distributions over future states, enabling richer uncertainty estimates. The 'point-wise' approach represents physical fields as point clouds, enabling application to large-scale systems. Yang serves as co-corresponding author, leading this emerging research direction.
What was presented at the APISAT 2021 international conference by Yang?

International Conference #1 — APISAT 2021 (Asia-Pacific International Symposium on Aerospace Technology) [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y.E. Kang, and K. Yee
- Title: Multi-fidelity optimization via regression-based hierarchical Kriging
- Summary: Presented a regression-based hierarchical Kriging method for multi-fidelity optimization. Hierarchical Kriging combines multiple levels of simulation fidelity into a unified surrogate model using Kriging (Gaussian process regression). This was Yang's first international conference presentation, occurring in 2021 during his PhD.
What was presented at the Asian Computational Fluid Dynamics Conference 2022 by Yang — the uncertainty quantification paper?

International Conference #2 — Asian Computational Fluid Dynamics Conference 2022 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Uncertainty Quantification via Deep Ensembles
- Summary: Presented the application of deep ensemble methods for uncertainty quantification in CFD-related regression tasks. This conference presentation preceded and informed the journal paper #6 (EAAI 2024). Yang presented the methodological foundation of using multiple neural network training runs to estimate predictive uncertainty in aerospace engineering contexts.
What was presented at the Asian Computational Fluid Dynamics Conference 2022 by Yang — the coaxial rotor paper?

International Conference #3 — Asian Computational Fluid Dynamics Conference 2022 [Oral]:
- Authors: Y. Hong, D. Lee, S. Yang, and K. Yee
- Title: Design Exploration on Performance for Co-rotating Coaxial Rotor
- Summary: Presented a design exploration study for co-rotating coaxial rotor configurations focusing on aerodynamic performance metrics. The study used surrogate-based design space exploration to systematically evaluate how rotor spacing, blade twist, and pitch affect hover efficiency. Yang contributed computational aerodynamic modeling and surrogate-based analysis.
What was presented at the 48th European Rotorcraft Forum 2022?

International Conference #4 — 48th European Rotorcraft Forum 2022 [Oral]:
- Authors: Y. Hong, D. Lee, S. Yang, and K. Yee
- Title: Numerical Investigation and Design Exploration on Aerodynamic Performance for Stacked Rotor
- Summary: Presented a comprehensive numerical investigation of stacked rotor aerodynamics, combining CFD analysis with design space exploration. The European Rotorcraft Forum is a premier international conference for rotorcraft research. This work formed part of the stacked rotor research strand that eventually culminated in journal paper #5 (Aerospace Science and Technology, 2023).
What was presented at APISAT 2022 by Yang?

International Conference #5 — Asia-Pacific International Symposium on Aerospace Technology (APISAT) 2022 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Quantifying Calibration Uncertainty in Missile Aerodynamic Data via Deep Ensembles
- Summary: Extended the deep ensemble UQ methodology to missile aerodynamic databases. 'Calibration' refers to whether the AI's expressed confidence actually matches its empirical accuracy. This paper quantified how well deep ensembles can produce calibrated uncertainty estimates for missile performance prediction, important for defense engineering where model reliability must be certified.
What was presented at AIAA SciTech 2023 by Yang?

International Conference #6 — AIAA SciTech 2023 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Uncertainty Quantification via Deep Ensembles in Missile Performance Prediction
- Summary: Presented at one of the largest aerospace engineering conferences (AIAA SciTech Forum, ~5000 attendees), this paper demonstrated deep ensemble uncertainty quantification specifically for missile performance prediction tasks. The AIAA presentation provided international visibility for Yang's UQ methodology, connecting the SNU aerospace engineering research to the global aerospace AI community.
What was presented at the 79th VFS Forum 2023?

International Conference #7 — 79th Annual Forum of the Vertical Flight Society (VFS) 2023 [Oral]:
- Authors: Y. Hong, D. Lee, S. Yang, H. Kook, and K. Yee
- Title: Design Exploration for Aerodynamic Performance of Hovering Stacked Rotor
- Summary: VFS Forum is the leading conference for helicopter and VTOL research. This paper extended the stacked rotor design exploration to focus specifically on hover performance, using surrogate-based methods to explore the effect of key design parameters. This work contributed to journal paper #5.
What was presented at AIAA Aviation 2024 by Yang?

International Conference #8 — AIAA Aviation Forum 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: Y.E. Kang, K. Lee, Y. Hong, S. Yang, and K. Yee
- Title: Leveraging Deep Neural Networks for Efficient Prediction of Aerodynamic Performance Tables
- Summary: Presented at AIAA Aviation 2024, a major US aerospace conference. The paper uses deep neural networks to build fast surrogate models for aerodynamic performance lookup tables — replacing time-consuming interpolation of tabulated CFD data with instant neural network inference. This work is closely related to journal paper #12 on proprotor optimization.
What was presented at ICTAM 2024 by Yang?

International Conference #9 — ICTAM (International Congress of Theoretical and Applied Mechanics) 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, R. Vinuesa, and N. Kang
- Title: Mesh-agnostic Spatio-temporal Prediction of Flows using Improved Graph U-nets
- Summary: ICTAM is a prestigious quadrennial congress of mechanics with ~3000 participants. Yang presented the improved Graph U-Net framework for mesh-agnostic spatio-temporal flow prediction — the foundational work for journal paper #13. The collaboration with Prof. Ricardo Vinuesa (KTH, Sweden) represents a strong international research connection.
What was presented at APS DFD 2024 by Yang?

International Conference #10 — APS Division of Fluid Dynamics (DFD) Annual Meeting 2024 [Interactive Session]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y. Hong, A. Vishwasrao, R. Vinuesa, and N. Kang
- Title: Integration of Temporal Dynamics in Graph U-Nets for Improved Mesh-Agnostic Spatio-Temporal Flow Prediction
- Summary: APS DFD is one of the world's largest fluid mechanics conferences (~4000 attendees). This interactive poster/oral session presented improvements to the Graph U-Net model by explicitly integrating temporal dynamics into the architecture, leading to better multi-step flow predictions. This work fed into journal paper #13.
What was presented at ASME IDETC/CIE 2025 by Yang?

International Conference #11 — ASME IDETC/CIE (International Design Engineering Technical Conferences) 2025 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Kim, S. Yang, M. Seo, and N. Kang
- Title: Decoupled Dynamics Framework with Neural Fields for 3D Spatio-Temporal Prediction of Vehicle Collisions
- Summary: ASME IDETC/CIE is the flagship design engineering conference of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers. This paper presented the neural-field-based decoupled dynamics framework for 3D vehicle collision prediction — the conference predecessor to journal paper #15 in Advanced Engineering Informatics (2026).
What was Yang's first NURETH-21 2025 paper about?

International Conference #12 — NURETH-21 (International Topical Meeting on Nuclear Reactor Thermal Hydraulics) 2025 [Oral]:
- Authors: J. Jeon, M. Lee, M. Song, B. Cho, S. Yang, N. Kang, H. Choi, Y. Yu
- Title: Preliminary CFD Benchmarking of Machine Learning Algorithm for Korean Virtual Small Modular Reactor
- Summary: Presented preliminary benchmarking of machine learning algorithms for simulating the Korean Virtual Small Modular Reactor (SMR) using CFD data. NURETH is the premier nuclear thermal-hydraulics conference. This work tests the feasibility of replacing computationally expensive reactor safety simulations with ML models, a critical step toward real-time digital twins of nuclear reactors.
What was Yang's second NURETH-21 2025 paper about?

International Conference #13 — NURETH-21 2025 [Oral]:
- Authors: J. Shin, M. Lee, C. Kim, S. Yang, J. Jeon
- Title: A Feasibility Study of Physics-Informed Neural Network-Based Severe Accident Analysis Code
- Summary: This paper studies the feasibility of using Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) as a replacement or surrogate for severe accident analysis codes in nuclear safety. Severe accidents (like core meltdowns) require complex multi-physics simulations; PINNs that embed governing equations could dramatically speed up safety assessments. This conference work directly preceded journal paper #14 in Progress in Nuclear Energy.
What was presented at APCOM 2025 by Yang?

International Conference #14 — APCOM (Asia-Pacific Congress on Computational Mechanics) 2025 [Oral]:
- Authors: J. Kim, S. Yang, and N. Kang
- Title: Point-based Diffusion Model for Predicting Spatio-Temporal Dynamics in Physical Systems
- Summary: The most recent international conference paper, presenting a point-based diffusion model for physical system dynamics prediction. Diffusion models (popularized by DALL-E and Stable Diffusion in image generation) are here adapted for physical simulation: given a point cloud representation of a physical system, the model predicts how the system evolves over time. This is the conference version of the under-review paper on point-wise diffusion models.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #1 (KSAS 2019)?

Domestic Conference #1 — KSAS (Korean Society for Aeronautical and Space Sciences) Spring Conference 2019 [Poster]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y. Hong, S. Park, and K. Yee
- Title: Multi-objective multi-fidelity design optimization of cranked wing type UCAV considering longitudinal stability
- Summary: Yang's very first conference presentation, during the early stages of his M.S. program. The paper applies multi-objective, multi-fidelity surrogate-based optimization to a cranked-wing UCAV planform, simultaneously minimizing radar cross-section and drag while maintaining longitudinal stability margins. This work laid the foundation for his M.S. thesis and subsequent UCAV design publications.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #2 (NCFE 2020)?

Domestic Conference #2 — NCFE (National Congress on Fluids Engineering) 2020 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Trade-off study of the multi-objective unmanned combat aerial vehicle optimization via variable-fidelity modeling and data mining
- Summary: This oral presentation explored trade-off relationships in multi-objective UCAV optimization using variable-fidelity (multi-fidelity) surrogate models combined with data mining techniques to extract design rules from the Pareto front. The work bridges engineering optimization and data analytics for aerodynamic design insights.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #3 (KSCFE 2020)?

Domestic Conference #3 — KSCFE (Korean Society for Computational Fluids Engineering) 2020 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, S. Park, and K. Yee
- Title: Multi-objective and multivariate aerodynamic design analysis of double delta wing UCAV
- Summary: Focused on the aerodynamic design analysis of double-delta wing UCAV configurations. Using computational fluid dynamics (CFD) and surrogate models, the study performed multi-objective optimization and multivariate analysis of how wing geometry parameters affect aerodynamic performance metrics (lift, drag, stability).
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #4 (KSAS 2020)?

Domestic Conference #4 — KSAS 2020 [Poster]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y. Hong, S. Park, and K. Yee
- Title: Airfoil optimization of UCAV considering cruise flight performance and low-speed longitudinal stability
- Summary: Extended UCAV design optimization to the airfoil level, optimizing airfoil geometry to balance cruise aerodynamic efficiency with low-speed stability requirements. This work used surrogate-based optimization to simultaneously satisfy competing aerodynamic objectives, relevant to the full-envelope performance requirements of modern UCAVs.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #5 (KSME 2020, Best Paper Award)?

Domestic Conference #5 — KSME (Korean Society of Mechanical Engineers) 2020 [Oral, Best Paper Award]:
- Authors: S. Shin, S. Yang, S. Lee, and K. Yee
- Title: Airfoil inverse design performance comparison among MLP, CNN, and RNN
- Summary: One of Yang's earliest Best Paper Award winners. This paper systematically benchmarks three neural network architectures — MLP (fully-connected), CNN (convolutional), and RNN (recurrent) — for the airfoil inverse design task (given target aerodynamic coefficients, generate the airfoil shape). It provides practical guidance on which architecture best suits geometric design prediction tasks in aerospace engineering.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #6 (KIIS 2021)?

Domestic Conference #6 — KIIS (Korean Institute of Intelligent Systems) 2021 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, S. Lee, and K. Yee
- Title: Development of inverse design framework using deep generative model
- Summary: Presented the development of an inverse design framework using deep generative models (specifically VAE-based architectures). Instead of iterative optimization, the generative model learns to directly map desired performance specifications to design geometries. This methodological work preceded the journal publication #4 (Engineering with Computers, 2023) on wind turbine airfoil inverse design.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #7 (KSME 2021)?

Domestic Conference #7 — KSME 2021 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y.E. Kang, and K. Yee
- Title: Multi-fidelity optimization via regression-based hierarchical Kriging
- Summary: The domestic version of the APISAT 2021 international paper. Presented the regression-based hierarchical Kriging framework for multi-fidelity optimization to the Korean mechanical engineering community. Hierarchical Kriging is a sophisticated Gaussian process method that explicitly models the correlation between different fidelity levels.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #8 (KSAS 2021)?

Domestic Conference #8 — KSAS 2021 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, S. Lee, and K. Yee
- Title: Inverse design optimization framework using variational autoencoder: application to wind turbine airfoil optimization
- Summary: Presented the VAE-based inverse design framework applied to wind turbine airfoils. The variational autoencoder learns a continuous latent space of airfoil shapes, enabling smooth interpolation and optimization in the latent space. Desired aerodynamic performance targets can be mapped back to actual airfoil geometry — a key step toward generative aerodynamic design.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #9 (KSAS 2021)?

Domestic Conference #9 — KSAS 2021 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, S. Yoo, S. Jeong, and K. Yee
- Title: Missile performance prediction via multi-fidelity modelling
- Summary: Applied multi-fidelity surrogate modeling to missile aerodynamic performance prediction, combining low-fidelity analytical codes with high-fidelity CFD simulations. Accurately predicting missile aerodynamic coefficients across a wide flight envelope is critical for flight simulation and control law development. This was one of Yang's early applications of multi-fidelity methods to defense-related engineering.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #10 (KSAS 2022)?

Domestic Conference #10 — KSAS 2022 [Poster]:
- Authors: Y.E. Kang, S. Yang, and K. Yee
- Title: Investigation on beta-VAE based reduced-order modeling of transonic flowfield
- Summary: The domestic conference version of the Physics of Fluids paper (journal #3). Presented preliminary β-VAE reduced-order modeling results for transonic flow to the Korean aerospace community. The β-VAE disentangles the latent representation of flow fields, enabling a compact but physically interpretable model of transonic phenomena like shock waves.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #11 (KSAS 2022)?

Domestic Conference #11 — KSAS 2022 [Poster]:
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Application of deep ensembles to quantifying predictive uncertainty in aerodynamic data
- Summary: Presented deep ensemble uncertainty quantification applied to aerodynamic database prediction. The study shows how training multiple neural networks with different random initializations produces well-calibrated uncertainty estimates for aerodynamic coefficients — enabling engineers to know which predictions to trust, a key capability for reliable AI-based aerodynamic design.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #12 (KSME 2022, Best Paper Award)?

Domestic Conference #12 — KSME 2022 [Oral, Best Paper Award]:
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Application of deep ensembles to quantifying predictive uncertainty in aerospace engineering
- Summary: Winner of a Best Paper Award. Extended the deep ensemble UQ methodology to broader aerospace engineering contexts beyond aerodynamics, including structural analysis and flight mechanics predictions. The paper demonstrates that deep ensembles provide more reliable uncertainty estimates than simpler alternatives (Monte Carlo dropout, single models with Gaussian output layers) for engineering regression tasks.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #13 (KIMT 2022)?

Domestic Conference #13 — KIMT (Korea Institute of Military Science and Technology) 2022 [Oral]:
- Authors: K. Kang, Y. Kim, E. Kang, J. Huh, S. Yang, and K. Yee
- Title: Comparison of fusion methods for modeling missile aerodynamic database based on multi-fidelity aerodynamic data
- Summary: A defense-focused paper comparing different multi-fidelity data fusion strategies for constructing missile aerodynamic databases. The study evaluates methods like bridge functions, co-kriging, and hierarchical kriging to determine which fusion approach best combines CFD data from different fidelity levels for missile coefficient prediction.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #14 (KDM 2022)?

Domestic Conference #14 — KDM (Korea Data Mining Society) 2022 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y.E. Kang, and K. Yee
- Title: Physics-aware Reduced-order Modeling of Transonic Flow via β-variational Autoencoder
- Summary: Presented the β-VAE physics-aware reduced-order modeling work to the data mining community — demonstrating how physics-aware machine learning connects to data science methodology. This cross-disciplinary presentation reflects Yang's ability to communicate aerospace AI research to broader AI/ML communities.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #15 (AIIS 2022)?

Domestic Conference #15 — AIIS (AI Innovation Symposium) 2022 [Poster]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y.E. Kang, and K. Yee
- Title: Extraction of Physical Generating Factors from Given Dataset
- Summary: An exploratory paper investigating methods to extract the underlying physical generating factors (independent physical variables) from observed engineering datasets. This relates to disentangled representation learning — understanding what physical quantities are actually driving variations in the dataset, without explicit labeling. This connects to the β-VAE work on learning interpretable latent representations.
What were Yang's domestic conference papers #16 and #17 (KSME 2022)?

Domestic Conferences #16 and #17 — KSME 2022 [Oral, two companion papers]:
- Paper #16: Authors: Y.E. Kang, S. Yang, and K. Yee | Title: Physics-aware prediction of high-dimensional data: practical applications
- Paper #17: Authors: S. Yang, Y.E. Kang, and K. Yee | Title: Physics-aware prediction of high-dimensional data: theoretical background
- Summary: These two companion papers (also presented at KSAS 2022 as #19 and #20) form a theoretical-practical pair. Paper #17/#20 establishes the mathematical and methodological foundations of physics-aware prediction for high-dimensional data. Paper #16/#19 demonstrates the practical engineering applications. Together they present a comprehensive framework for physics-guided dimensionality reduction and prediction.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #18 (KSAS 2022)?

Domestic Conference #18 — KSAS 2022 [Poster]:
- Authors: S. Yang and K. Yee
- Title: Towards Quantifying Calibrated Uncertainty in Missile Performance Regression Tasks via Deep Ensembles
- Summary: Presented research on calibration of uncertainty estimates in missile performance prediction — ensuring that when the AI says it is 90% confident, it is actually correct 90% of the time. Calibration is essential for safety-critical defense applications where overconfident AI predictions can lead to poor design decisions or mission failures.
What were Yang's domestic conference papers #19 and #20 (KSAS 2022)?

Domestic Conference #19 and #20 — KSAS 2022 [Oral, two companion papers]:
- Paper #19: Authors: Y.E. Kang, S. Yang, and K. Yee | Title: Physics-aware prediction of high-dimensional data: practical applications
- Paper #20: Authors: S. Yang, Y.E. Kang, and K. Yee | Title: Physics-aware prediction of high-dimensional data: theoretical background
- Summary: The KSAS versions of the KSME 2022 companion papers (#16 and #17). Presenting the same physics-aware high-dimensional prediction framework to the aerospace community. Showing these results at both KSME and KSAS demonstrates the cross-disciplinary applicability of the methodology.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #21 (KSCFE 2023)?

Domestic Conference #21 — KSCFE 2023 [Oral]:
- Authors: Y. Hong, D. Lee, S. Yang, H. Kook, and K. Yee
- Title: Investigation of Steady and Unsteady effects of Hovering Stacked Rotor
- Summary: Analyzed both steady-state and unsteady (time-varying) aerodynamic effects of hovering stacked rotor systems. Unsteady effects (blade vortex interaction, wake dynamics) significantly affect hover efficiency and noise. This CFD study quantifies the relative importance of unsteady phenomena for stacked rotor design optimization.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #22 (KSCFE 2023)?

Domestic Conference #22 — KSCFE 2023 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, H. Kim, Y. Hong, K. Yee, and N. Kang
- Title: Prediction of 2D Flow Field using Vorticity-pressure-aware Physics-informed Neural Networks
- Summary: Introduced PINNs that explicitly incorporate vorticity-pressure relationships (derived from Navier-Stokes equations) into the loss function. Traditional PINNs use continuity and momentum equations; this enhancement adds vorticity transport to improve prediction of rotational flow features around bodies. This marks Yang's transition from surrogate modeling to physics-informed neural networks.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #23 (KSME 2023, Best Paper Award)?

Domestic Conference #23 — KSME 2023 [Oral, Best Paper Award]:
- Authors: S. Yang, H. Kim, Y. Hong, K. Yee, and N. Kang
- Title: Fluid-oriented Physics-informed Neural Networks via Adaptive Sampling and Data-driven Approaches
- Summary: Won the Best Paper Award. Extended fluid-oriented PINNs with two key innovations: (1) adaptive sampling — dynamically placing collocation points in regions of high residual loss where physics are most violated, and (2) data-driven initialization — using existing CFD data to provide better starting points for PINN training. These improvements significantly accelerate PINN convergence for complex fluid dynamics problems.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #24 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #24 — KSME 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: D. Lee, S. Yang, J. Oh, S. Cho, S. Kim, and N. Kang
- Title: Digital Twin for Wave Energy Converter: Uncertainty Quantification of Real-time Wave Height Prediction using Deep Learning
- Summary: Presented a digital twin framework for wave energy converters that uses deep learning with uncertainty quantification to predict wave heights in real time. Wave energy converters extract energy from ocean waves, and reliable real-time wave prediction enables better control strategies. This work is related to the under-review journal paper on AI-powered ocean digital twin.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #25 (KSME 2024, Best Paper Award)?

Domestic Conference #25 — KSME 2024 [Oral, Best Paper Award]:
- Authors: S. Lee, S. Yang, and N. Kang
- Title: Manipulator Mechanism Design Optimization using Surrogate Model-based Optimization and Sensitivity Analysis for Design Rule Extraction Methodology
- Summary: Won the Best Paper Award. This paper applies surrogate model-based optimization to manipulator mechanism design, extracting design rules through sensitivity analysis. Design rules are interpretable guidelines (e.g., 'increasing link length by 10% improves reach by 8%') that help engineers understand and communicate design insights beyond just finding the optimal solution. Related to journal paper #10.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #26 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #26 — KSME 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: J. Kim, J. Park, S. Yang, and N. Kang
- Title: Physics-Informed Graph Neural Network Based Surrogate Model for Predicting the Durability of OLED Displays under Drop Impact
- Summary: Presented the physics-informed GNN surrogate model for OLED display drop impact prediction. The model combines graph neural networks (which naturally handle mesh-based structural simulations) with physics constraints (stress equilibrium, contact mechanics) to predict whether OLED panels will crack or delaminate under specified drop conditions. Conference version of journal paper #8.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #27 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #27 — KSME 2024 [Poster]:
- Authors: S. Kim, S. Yang, and N.W. Kang
- Title: Feasibility Study of Multi-fidelity LSTM for Time-series Prediction
- Summary: Investigated the feasibility of combining multi-fidelity data strategies with LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) networks for time-series prediction of physical systems. LSTM networks capture temporal dependencies, and multi-fidelity methods reduce the data requirement. This work explores whether the multi-fidelity approach used in DeepONet and Kriging can be extended to recurrent architectures.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #28 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #28 — KSME 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, R. Vinuesa, and N. Kang
- Title: Temporal Flow Fields Prediction using Mesh-agnostic Graph U-Nets
- Summary: Presented the mesh-agnostic Graph U-Net for temporal flow field prediction to the Korean mechanical engineering community. The graph U-Net architecture combines U-Net's multi-scale feature extraction with GNN's mesh-agnostic property, enabling accurate prediction of how flow fields evolve over consecutive time steps around arbitrary geometries.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #29 (KSCFE 2024)?

Domestic Conference #29 — KSCFE 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, R. Vinuesa, and N. Kang
- Title: Prediction of Vortex Shedding from a Circular Cylinder using Graph Neural Networks
- Summary: Focused on the canonical fluid dynamics benchmark of vortex shedding from a circular cylinder (Kármán vortex street). Graph neural networks are used to predict the periodic, alternating vortex patterns that develop in the wake of a cylinder — a classic test case for AI-based flow solvers. Presented to the Korean CFD community.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #30 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #30 — KSME 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, R. Vinuesa, and N.W. Kang
- Title: Temporal Flow Fields Prediction using Mesh-agnostic Graph U-Nets
- Summary: A parallel presentation of the Graph U-Net temporal flow prediction work at KSME (same research presented at both KSME and KSCFE conferences in 2024, reaching different audiences in the Korean engineering community). Emphasizes the temporal aspect of the predictions — autoregressive rollout over multiple time steps.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #31 (KSCFE 2024)?

Domestic Conference #31 — KSCFE 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, R. Vinuesa, and N. Kang
- Title: Prediction of Vortex Shedding from a Circular Cylinder using Graph Neural Networks
- Summary: The KSCFE version of the circular cylinder vortex shedding GNN paper (same as #29). Presented at both KSME-affiliated and KSCFE conferences to maximize reach within the Korean CFD and mechanical engineering research community.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #32 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #32 — KSME 2024 [Poster]:
- Authors: S. Kim, S. Yang, and N.W. Kang
- Title: Improvement and Optimization of Ensemble-based Multi-Fidelity Approaches
- Summary: Investigated improvements to ensemble-based multi-fidelity surrogate modeling, combining ensemble learning (multiple surrogate models) with multi-fidelity data fusion. The study compares different ensemble strategies and proposes optimization criteria for selecting ensemble weights, addressing how to best combine predictions from models trained on data of different accuracy levels.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #33 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #33 — KSME 2024 [Poster]:
- Authors: J. Kim, S. Yang, and N.W. Kang
- Title: Comparison of Spatio-Temporal Prediction Performance of 2D Cylinder Flow: Meshgraphnet vs Neural Implicit Representation
- Summary: A benchmark study comparing two AI architectures for spatio-temporal flow prediction around a 2D cylinder: MeshGraphNet (a GNN-based approach from Google DeepMind) and neural implicit representations (coordinate-based neural networks). This comparison helps identify which approach is more suitable for different aspects of flow prediction accuracy, generalization, and computational cost.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #34 (KSME 2024)?

Domestic Conference #34 — KSME 2024 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y. Wang, A. Vishwasrao, R. Vinuesa, and N.W. Kang
- Title: Improved Temporal Prediction of Transient Flow Fields using Graph Neural Networks
- Summary: Presented improvements to GNN-based temporal prediction of transient (time-varying) flow fields. Key innovations include better temporal encoding and multi-step loss functions that penalize error accumulation during autoregressive rollout. Co-authored with Y. Wang (Korean collaborator) and A. Vishwasrao, showing growing team collaboration around the Graph U-Net research thread.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #35 (AAICON 2025, Best Paper Award)?

Domestic Conference #35 — AAICON 2025 [Oral, Best Paper Award]:
- Authors: S. Kim, S. Yang, S. Kim, J. Han, B. Cho, C. Song, J. Kang, and N.W. Kang
- Title: Prediction of Unsteady Flow Fields for Varying Multi-Cylinder Arrangements Using DeepONet
- Summary: Won the Best Paper Award. Uses Deep Operator Networks (DeepONet) to predict unsteady flow fields for different arrangements of multiple cylinders — varying number, spacing, and layout. DeepONet learns a function-to-function mapping: given a cylinder arrangement as input, it outputs the complete flow field. This has applications in heat exchanger design and pipe flow engineering.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #36 (KSME 2025)?

Domestic Conference #36 — KSME 2025 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang and N.W. Kang
- Title: AI Agents for Multi Fidelity Aerodynamic Shape Optimization
- Summary: Presented an innovative approach using AI agents (LLM-based or RL-based autonomous agents) to automate multi-fidelity aerodynamic shape optimization workflows. Instead of a human engineer manually coordinating low- and high-fidelity simulations, AI agents autonomously decide which fidelity to query, how to update the surrogate, and when to terminate optimization. This connects to the KARI invited talk on 'LLM Agents for Drone Design.'
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #37 (KSAS 2025)?

Domestic Conference #37 — KSAS 2025 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang and N.W. Kang
- Title: Multi Fidelity Aerodynamic Shape Optimization using AI Agents
- Summary: The KSAS aerospace version of the AI agents aerodynamic optimization paper. Presented to the Korean aerospace community, emphasizing the aerospace application context — using AI agents to autonomously manage the complex multi-fidelity CFD workflow for airfoil and wing shape optimization, a traditionally human-guided iterative process.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #38 (KSME 2025)?

Domestic Conference #38 — KSME 2025 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, Y. Lee, and N.W. Kang
- Title: Physics Guided Multi Fidelity DeepONet for Data Efficient Flow Field Prediction
- Summary: Presented the physics-guided multi-fidelity DeepONet framework for data-efficient flow field prediction. This work explicitly encodes physical constraints into a multi-fidelity DeepONet, combining physics-informed training with multi-fidelity data fusion for maximum data efficiency. The conference version of journal paper #9 (CMAME 2025).
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #39 (KSAS 2026)?

Domestic Conference #39 — KSAS 2026 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, S. Park, and K. Yee
- Title: Aerodynamic Design Optimization of Martian Rotorcraft Airfoil Using Generative AI and Quality-Diversity Algorithms
- Summary: Combines generative AI with quality-diversity (QD) algorithms for Martian rotorcraft airfoil design. QD algorithms (like MAP-Elites) maintain a diverse archive of high-quality solutions rather than converging to a single optimum — valuable for design exploration. The Martian application is challenging due to the extremely low atmospheric density (about 1% of Earth's), requiring specially designed airfoils for sufficient lift.
What was Yang's domestic conference paper #40 (KSME 2026)?

Domestic Conference #40 — KSME 2026 [Oral]:
- Authors: S. Yang, S. Park, and K. Yee
- Title: Aerodynamic Design Optimization of Martian Rotorcraft Airfoil using AI
- Summary: The KSME version of the Martian rotorcraft airfoil design paper. Presented to the Korean mechanical engineering community, this paper demonstrates AI-based aerodynamic shape optimization for the unique challenge of operating in the Martian atmosphere — where Reynolds numbers are extremely low and airfoil design conventions from Earth do not directly apply. Related to the KSME CAE Division invited talk in May 2026.
What is IDEA LAB?

IDEA LAB (Intelligent Design for Engineering Applications Lab) is a research laboratory at the Department of Mechanical Engineering, Hanyang University ERICA, founded on March 1, 2026 by Assistant Professor Sunwoong Yang. The lab develops physics-guided AI methods that accelerate engineering simulation and design, with applications spanning aerospace, automotive, nuclear, manufacturing, and ocean engineering. Lab slogan: 'We shape IDEAs into IDEAL design solutions. And AI is our design engine.'
What is IDEA LAB's research philosophy?

IDEA LAB pursues two core research transitions: (1) data-driven AI → knowledge-driven AI: embedding domain expert knowledge so models understand real production constraints; (2) physics-blind AI → physics-guided AI: enforcing physical laws so predictions are reliable and explainable. The goal is to move from ideas to ideal designs, with applications from ground (automotive, manufacturing) to space (aerospace, Mars exploration).
What are IDEA LAB's research keywords?

IDEA LAB's five core research keywords are: (1) AI-based Simulation, (2) Physics-guided AI, (3) Long-term Forecasting, (4) Uncertainty Quantification, (5) Generative Design. Together, these cover the full workflow from AI-accelerated simulation through reliable prediction to design optimization.
What projects is IDEA LAB currently running?

IDEA LAB has three ongoing projects (all PI: Prof. Yang): (1) KAI-funded UAV aerodynamic AI framework, 20M KRW, 2026–2027; (2) NRF Sejong Fellowship — AI flow prediction for digital twins, 500M KRW, 2024–2029; (3) Hanyang University — AI aerodynamic design of Mars helicopter blades, 40M KRW, 2026–2027. One completed project: KITECH blow molding AI, 20M KRW (2024, 4 months).
IDEA LAB에서는 어떤 연구를 하나요?

IDEA LAB은 물리 기반 AI(Physics-Guided AI)를 활용한 공학 시뮬레이션 및 설계 자동화 연구를 수행합니다. 주요 연구 영역은 (1) AI 기반 시뮬레이션 가속화, (2) 장기 시계열 예측(Long-term Forecasting), (3) 불확실성 정량화(Uncertainty Quantification), (4) 생성 설계(Generative Design), (5) 물리 법칙을 내재화한 AI 모델링입니다. 항공우주, 자동차, 원자력, 제조, 해양 등 다양한 분야에 적용합니다.
양선웅 교수는 어떤 사람인가요?

양선웅 교수는 한양대학교 ERICA 기계공학과 조교수로, 2026년 3월 IDEA LAB을 창설했습니다. 서울대학교 항공우주공학과에서 박사학위(우수박사학위논문상 수상, 2023)를 받고, KAIST 세종펠로우십 박사후연구원(2023–2026)을 거쳐 현직에 임용되었습니다. 공학 설계를 위한 물리 기반 AI — 물리 법칙과 도메인 지식을 내재화한 AI 모델 개발 — 을 전문으로 하며, SCI(E) 저널 15편, 국내외 학회 54회의 발표 실적을 보유합니다.
양선웅 교수의 학력은 어떻게 되나요?

양선웅 교수의 학력: 서울대학교 기계항공공학부 학사(2014.03–2018.02) → 서울대학교 기계항공공학부 석사(2018.03–2020.02, 지도교수: 이관중 교수, 석사논문: 가변 충실도를 활용한 종방향 안정성 및 저피탐지성을 고려한 무인전투기 평면형상 최적화) → 서울대학교 항공우주공학과 박사(2020.03–2023.08, 지도교수: 이관중 교수, 박사논문: 데이터 기반 접근법을 활용한 효율적 공력 설계, 우수박사학위논문상 수상).
양선웅 교수의 경력은 어떻게 되나요?

양선웅 교수의 경력: 서울대학교 박사 졸업(2023.08) → KAIST 모빌리티 대학원 박사후연구원(2023.09–2026.02, 지도교수: 강남우 교수, 세종펠로우십 수혜) → 한양대학교 ERICA 기계공학과 조교수(2026.03~현재, IDEA LAB 창설). 산학협력: 한국항공우주산업(KAI), 한국생산기술연구원(KITECH), 한국원자력연구원 관련 기관 등과 공동 연구 수행.
양선웅 교수의 연구 분야가 기존 공학과 어떻게 다른가요?

기존 공학 연구자는 수치 시뮬레이션(CFD, FEM)에 의존하거나, 일반 AI 연구자는 데이터만으로 모델을 훈련합니다. 양선웅 교수는 이 둘을 통합합니다. 물리 방정식을 AI 모델에 내재화하여 (1) 데이터가 부족해도 신뢰할 수 있는 예측이 가능하고, (2) 물리적으로 말이 안 되는 결과를 자동으로 배제하며, (3) 예측 불확실성을 정량화하여 엔지니어의 신뢰도 판단을 돕습니다. 이는 단순한 AI 활용을 넘어 공학 현장을 위한 AI 설계입니다.
What are Sunwoong Yang's Best Paper Award winning papers?

Yang has won 5 Best Paper Awards at Korean domestic conferences:
1. KSME 2020: 'Airfoil inverse design performance comparison among MLP, CNN, and RNN' (with S. Shin, S. Lee, K. Yee)
2. KSME 2022: 'Application of deep ensembles to quantifying predictive uncertainty in aerospace engineering' (with K. Yee)
3. KSME 2023: 'Fluid-oriented Physics-informed Neural Networks via Adaptive Sampling and Data-driven Approaches' (with H. Kim, Y. Hong, K. Yee, N. Kang)
4. KSME 2024: 'Manipulator Mechanism Design Optimization using Surrogate Model-based Optimization' (with S. Lee, N. Kang)
5. AAICON 2025: 'Prediction of Unsteady Flow Fields for Varying Multi-Cylinder Arrangements Using DeepONet' (with S. Kim et al.)
What is the connection between Sunwoong Yang and Prof. Ricardo Vinuesa?

Prof. Ricardo Vinuesa (KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Sweden) is an international collaborator of Yang. They co-authored three publications: journal paper #11 (JCDE, 2025) on long-term fluid dynamics prediction, journal paper #13 (ESWA, 2026) on mesh-agnostic Graph U-Net flow prediction, and conference papers at ICTAM 2024 and APS DFD 2024. Vinuesa is a leading researcher in AI for turbulence and fluid mechanics, and this collaboration brings international visibility to IDEA LAB's flow prediction research.

Park and K. Yee); (3) KSAS 2026 [Oral] — 'Aerodynamic Design Optimization of Martian Rotorcraft Airfoil Using Generative AI and Quality-Diversity Algorithms' (with S. Park and K. Yee).
How does Sunwoong Yang approach uncertainty quantification in engineering AI?

Yang approaches UQ primarily through deep ensembles — training multiple neural networks with different random initializations and aggregating their predictions. He has published multiple works on this: journal paper #6 (EAAI 2024) establishing the theoretical framework for calibrated UQ in multi-output regression, journal paper #7 (CMAME 2024) applying it in PINNs digital twin contexts, and conference papers on missile aerodynamics and aerodynamic databases. He evaluates UQ through calibration metrics (reliability diagrams, expected calibration error) rather than just predictive accuracy, ensuring AI systems know what they don't know.
What is the significance of Yang's work on graph neural networks for flow prediction?

Yang's GNN work addresses a fundamental limitation of traditional AI flow surrogates: they are mesh-dependent, meaning a model trained on one mesh topology cannot generalize to a different mesh. Yang's Graph U-Net approach (paper #13, ESWA 2026) and related conference papers achieve mesh-agnostic prediction — the GNN operates on graph-structured data representing any mesh, enabling zero-shot generalization to new geometries or mesh refinements. This is critical for practical engineering workflows where geometry changes continuously during design iterations.
How can I join IDEA LAB?

To join IDEA LAB, send an email to sunwoongy@hanyang.ac.kr with your area of interest, academic transcripts, and portfolio (CV). Tracks available: Integrated M.S./Ph.D. (min. 6 months undergraduate research recommended), M.S. (min. 3 months recommended), Ph.D., and Undergraduate Researcher (3rd year+). No prior AI experience required. The lab targets research in engineering simulation/design automation across aerospace, defense, manufacturing, automotive, fuel cells, and related fields.
What benefits does IDEA LAB provide?

IDEA LAB benefits include: industry-academia project portfolio (automotive, semiconductor, aerospace, defense), paper mentoring toward SCI(E) publication within 2 years, full conference support (domestic + international), collaborative research with SNU/KAIST/POSTECH/DLR/Purdue/Michigan, 130,000 KRW/month AI subscription support (ChatGPT, Claude, Google AI Pro), paper incentives, project incentives, core hours 10am–4pm, and coffee machine.
What is the culture of IDEA LAB?

IDEA LAB emphasizes open communication, rapid execution, and collaborative innovation. As a newly established lab led by a young professor, the culture is flat and dynamic — members help shape the lab's direction together. Prof. Yang provides systematic coaching and a clear research roadmap to prevent research drift. The lab encourages sharing trial-and-error experiences. Students are matched to research topics based on their career goals, with Prof. Yang directly arranging collaborations with industry and academic partners.
What is IDEA LAB's full name?

IDEA LAB stands for 'Intelligent Design for Engineering Applications Laboratory.' The name is also a conceptual wordplay: 'IDEA' refers to creative design ideas that the lab shapes into 'IDEAL' engineering solutions, as reflected in the lab slogan: 'We shape IDEAs into IDEAL design solutions.'

3. May 7, 2026 — Prof. Yang delivered an invited talk at the KSME CAE Division Conference 2026 in Yeosu, Korea, on 'Aerodynamic Design Optimization of Martian Rotorcraft Airfoil.'
